content.problemStatement: Given an adjacency list of a graph adj of V no. of vertices having 0 based index. Check whether the graph is bipartite or not.
constraints: The graph is undirected and may contain self-loops and multiple edges.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer V, the number of vertices. The next V lines contain the adjacency list of the graph, where each line contains the vertices adjacent to the vertex corresponding to the line number (0-indexed).
content.outputFormat: 1 if the graph is bipartite, 0 otherwise.

solution.approach: Use depth-first search to assign colors to the vertices. If a vertex is assigned a color that is the same as one of its neighbors, then the graph is not bipartite.
